title: hwid buffer undersized for some MCUs
description:
In lora_l2.c generate_eui64(), the hwid buffer is 16 bytes, but some MCUs have larger hardware IDs.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:84

Code:
- Line 84: uint8_t hwid[16];
- Line 87: hwinfo_get_device_id(hwid, sizeof(hwid));

What goes wrong: hwinfo_get_device_id() returns the number of bytes available or -errno. If a future MCU has >16 byte hwid, the function truncates silently - it copies up to sizeof(hwid) bytes and returns the truncated count. Two MCUs with hwids that differ only after byte 16 would hash to identical EUI-64s.

Current Zephyr-supported MCUs:
- STM32: 12 bytes
- nRF52: 8 bytes  
- ESP32: 6 bytes
- All fit in 16 bytes

This is a latent bug that would only manifest on future hardware. The SHA-256 hash would still produce a valid EUI-64, but collision probability increases if entropy is truncated.

Fix: Increase buffer to 32 bytes (Zephyr's HWINFO_DEVID_MAX_LEN is 16, but better to be defensive), or add a static_assert that sizeof(hwid) >= expected max.
